articles.title: Defining subcellular synovial responses in TMJ osteoarthritis onset via mechanical stress and articular disk derangement models
articles.summary: Temporomandibular joint osteoarthritis (TMJ-OA), the most common degenerative disease of the TMJ, is influenced by various adaptive, inflammatory, and mechanical stressors. a transcriptomic approach combines subcellular spatial transcriptomics and single-cell RNA sequencing in murine models of mechanical stresses and articular disk derangement. cell type–and cluster–specific catabolic changes were observed under these stress conditions,

articles.title: Integration of gold standard arthroscopy, MRI and synovial histopathology outcomes for enhancing early knee osteoarthritis diagnosis
articles.summary: arthroscopy, MRI, and synovial histopathology were used to characterize early KOA symptoms. the study included 130 adults (> 18 years) with knee pain lasting > 3 months and inconclusive clinical and radiographic findings. isolated minor findings (small focal cartilage defects or mild meniscal changes without structural disruption)

articles.title: Long-term functional and clinical outcomes of intraarticular double-cross-linked high molecular weight hyaluronic acid (Crespine Gel Plus) injection in knee osteoarthritis: a one year prospective study
articles.summary: double cross-linked, high-molecular-weight hyaluronic acid (HMWHA; Crespine Gel Plus) may extend symptom relief beyond the typical 3 to 6 month horizon. robust 12-month data remain limited, but short- and long-term outcomes over 12 months following a single mL intra-articular injection in routine care. results were KOOS4 (primary; 0–100, higher = better

articles.title: Metabolic dysfunction-associated fatty liver disease and risk of knee osteoarthritis: A prospective cohort study
articles.summary: fatty liver disease (MAFLD) is a systemic metabolic disorder that influences extra-hepatic conditions. cross-sectional studies link the disease to KOA, but prospective evidence remains limited. this study included 303,604 participants without baseline osteoarthritis.
